title: Have info print conmon/oci runtime information
body:
We need into to identify the OCI runtime and conmon used by podman.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@baude PTAL this is a followon to your earlier patch to add path and package version.
@vrothberg Could you tell me how to implement the equivalent of rpm -qf /usr/bin/runc with apt?
@lsm5 if you know how to do this with apt, I would appreciate your input also.

@rhatdan For Debian-based distributions you can use `dpkg -S $PATH`. However, I am a bit concerned about this PR as distributions may choose different paths (e.g., `/usr/sbin/runc` on openSUSE). I would personally prefer to execute something like `runc --version` and parse that.

*update*: conmon on openSUSE is at `/usr/lib/crio/bin/conmon`.
@rhatdan scratch my previous comments regarding different paths. I should have had a closer look at the code, and as we're getting the paths from the runtime, everything looks good to me.
@vrothberg Added a patch for dpkg support

I'm a little hesitant to start going through package versions manually - where do we stop? Arch is a popular distro, should we be including pacman in there?

LGTM, but I remain hesitant.
Please wait before merge, want to test on Debian/Ubuntu before.
@rhatdan I tested it via `make shell` and found that we need to check for `!= "Unknown"` to make it work. Once that's changed, feel free to merge.
take it or leave it comment, else LGTM

I will leave it up to a someone from Arch to ask for this.  I think this information is critical to make debugging issues easier.  And when this gets into RHEL for support people to know what people are using.

:pushpin: Commit 32cb946 has been approved by `baude`
